31.reason: Error: This condition prevents handling '-' at index 0. A leading '-' should be printed as is.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: This condition prevents handling '-' at index 0. A leading '-' should be printed as is.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  If the input string starts with '-', i is 0, causing an infinite loop since i is never incremented. Also fails to print '-' when expansion conditions are not met.

33.reason: Error: Missing check that str[i-1] < str[i+1]; also does not handle case where str[i+1] is null terminator (end of string) resulting in the '-' being omitted.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: Missing check that str[i-1] < str[i+1]; also does not handle case where str[i+1] is null terminator (end of string) resulting in the '-' being omitted.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Missing check for str[i-1] < str[i+1] as required by the problem. Also lacks an else clause to print '-' when expansion conditions are not satisfied, causing '-' to be omitted from the output.

40.reason: Error: When expansion is not performed (ifproper false or left>=right), the '-' character is not printed. This line should be preceded by an else that prints the '-' as is.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: When expansion is not performed (ifproper false or left>=right), the '-' character is not printed. This line should be preceded by an else that prints the '-' as is.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Incrementing i here without printing '-' when conditions fail leads to missing '-' in the output. The logic should print '-' in an else block before incrementing.

22.reason: The gets function is deprecated and has been removed from the C11 standard, causing a compilation error in strict C11 or later compilers. Also, it is unsafe and can lead to buffer overflow.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  The gets function is deprecated and has been removed from the C11 standard, causing a compilation error in strict C11 or later compilers. Also, it is unsafe and can lead to buffer overflow.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  gets() is deprecated and removed in modern C standards (C11/C23), which causes compilation errors in strict environments. Use fgets() instead.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Using gets() is unsafe and may cause compilation errors or warnings in modern compilers; it was removed in C11 standard. Use fgets() instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Using 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; may cause compilation error with modern compilers due to removal from standard library]
